paper_identity.title: DiskANN: Fast Accurate Billion-point Nearest Neighbor Search on a Single Node

scientific_problem.problem: High infrastructure costs due to state-of-the-art ANNS graph algorithms requiring complete index storage in main memory RAM.
scientific_problem.motivation: Enabling billion-point vector similarity search on a single workstation under standard memory constraints without loss of recall precision.
scientific_problem.gap: Traditional memory-efficient approaches (inverted indexing + compression) suffer from significant lossy precision drops, while standard high-precision graph methods cannot scale to billions of points on a single limited node.
scientific_problem.hypothesis: A structured graph with low diameter and long-range routing capability can effectively execute concurrent low-latency lookups on solid-state drives using compact memory routing guides.
scientific_problem.research_question: Can an SSD-resident index achieve high-recall, low query latency, and high point density per node simultaneously?
scientific_problem.success_criteria: Serving >5000 QPS with <5ms mean latency and over 95% 1-recall@1 on a single 64GB RAM node.

methods.name: Vamana Graph Construction
methods.role: Global indexing algorithm optimizing degree and graph diameter using structured iterative multi-pass traversal.
methods.input: Base point matrix, alpha pruning factor, graph degree limit R.
methods.claimed_advantage: Logarithmic convergence paths due to long-range connectivity injection via alpha tuning.
methods.limitation: Requires full data access passes, increasing initial indexing build pipelines.

methods.name: DiskANN Beam Search
methods.role: Parallel look-ahead graph exploration strategy utilizing concurrent asynchronous disk hardware queues.
methods.input: Query embedding, beam width W, starting node medoid.
methods.claimed_advantage: Saves processing overhead by compressing independent disk rounds into grouped hardware IOPS operations.
methods.limitation: High beam configurations trigger operational computational waste.

metrics.name: 1-recall@1
metrics.definition: Proportion of queries where the single extracted candidate is the actual closest geometric neighbor.

metrics.name: Mean Latency
metrics.definition: Average time spent executing a distinct similarity search task.
